name: notifications-baseline
description: Multi-channel notification service: web (in-app inbox), email, and SMS. Tenants register message templates with locale-keyed bodies and a placeholder language for runtime substitution. Per-user channel opt-ins live in the Preferences service; sends honour them.

attributes.summary: Multi-channel notification dispatch (web / email / SMS) with tenant-defined message templates, localisation, and per-user channel opt-ins honoured at send time.

endpoints.summary: Send one notification to one user on one or more channels.

email_override.description: If set, overrides the user's stored email. Subject to audit + admin role.

channels.description: Channels to send on. Channels the user has opted out of are silently skipped (audit recorded).

variables.description: Substitution values for the template's `{{placeholder}}` tokens.

locale.description: Render template in this locale. Defaults to the user's `language` preference.

priority.description: Critical bypasses some opt-out checks (security alerts only).

endpoints.summary: Bulk send. Up to 10,000 recipients per request. Same template; per-recipient variables allowed.
List the templates this tenant has registered (plus platform-shared templates if any).
Create a notification template. Requires the `admin` role.

body.description: Body template. For email, supports Markdown. For SMS, plain text only (160-char soft limit, hard fail at 1600).

body_html.description: Email-only. Pre-rendered HTML alternative to Markdown.

placeholders.description: Declared placeholder schema. /send validates submitted variables against this list.

endpoints.summary: Update a template. Updates create a new immutable version; the active version pointer moves on success.

request.schema: Same as POST, all fields optional

endpoints.summary: Render a template against sample variables without sending. Useful for the admin UI preview pane.
List the user's web-channel inbox (unread + read). Order: most recent first.
Mark an inbox message as read. Idempotent.
Lookup a single delivery's status — useful for the email/SMS provider's webhook acks.

template_language.name: handlebars-subset
template_language.summary: Mustache-style `{{placeholder}}` substitution. Conditionals (`{{#if foo}}...{{/if}}`) and iteration (`{{#each items}}...{{/each}}`) are supported. Helpers: `{{format_date var locale="en-US"}}`, `{{format_currency var ccy="USD"}}`. No raw HTML in plain-text channels. Email channel optionally accepts `body_html` for full HTML escape-hatch.

channel_quirks.web: In-app inbox. Stored indefinitely until the user deletes. Read receipts via /inbox/{id}/read. Renders Markdown.
channel_quirks.email: Provider abstraction over SendGrid + SES (configurable per-tenant). Bounces tracked via provider webhooks. Unsubscribe link auto-appended when channel opt-in is user-controllable.
channel_quirks.sms: Provider abstraction over Twilio + AWS SNS. 160-char soft limit, 1600-char hard limit (concatenated SMS). E.164 phone format. No marketing content allowed — only transactional + critical.

facts.title: Overview
facts.body: Notifications is a multi-channel dispatch service: web (in-app inbox), email, and SMS. Senders send by `(user_id, template_id, channels[])` — the service handles locale resolution, placeholder substitution, opt-in honouring, and provider routing. Templates are tenant-scoped, versioned, and locale-keyed. Per-user channel preferences live in the Preferences service (`notification_opt_ins.{email,sms,web}`) and are honoured at send time — opted-out channels are silently skipped with an audit entry.

facts.title: Authoring a notification template
facts.body: Templates declare their placeholder schema upfront. At send time, the service validates submitted variables against that schema — missing required placeholders fail with HTTP 422, no notification dispatched. The template language is a handlebars subset: `{{name}}`, `{{#if condition}}…{{/if}}`, `{{#each items}}…{{/each}}`, plus formatting helpers (`{{format_date}}`, `{{format_currency}}`). For email, the `body` field accepts Markdown and is rendered server-side; supply `body_html` if you need full HTML control. Templates are created in `draft` status — promote to `active` once you've tested via `POST /v1/notifications/templates/{id}/render`.

facts.title: Provider configuration per tenant
facts.body: Per-tenant provider selection lives in a Notifications admin endpoint (not user-callable). Tenants can pick SendGrid or SES for email; Twilio or AWS SNS for SMS. Provider credentials are stored in the platform secret store (referenced by name, never plaintext in the DB). The web channel has no third-party provider — messages land directly in the user's inbox via the inbox-write path.

facts.title: Security model
facts.body: All endpoints require Identity-issued tokens. `POST /v1/notifications/send` requires either the `producer` role (sending to users in your own tenant) or admin (sending to users in any tenant — rare; cross-tenant sends are audited and rate-limited). `email_override` and `phone_override` on the send body require `admin` and emit a high-priority audit entry. Template bodies are sanitised — HTML-tag stripping for SMS, script-tag stripping + URL-allowlisting for email HTML. Critical-priority sends (security alerts) bypass channel opt-out but ARE NOT a marketing escape hatch — abuse triggers tenant-level rate-limit + audit alert.

properties.reason: Bearer-token auth on every endpoint; user-id resolution for sends.
Reads `notification_opt_ins.{web,email,sms}` per recipient at send time to honour opt-outs.
